sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery is the primary curative treatment for penile cancer, aiming to achieve negative margins while preserving penile function when feasible. It includes organ-sparing approaches for early-stage disease and more extensive procedures for advanced tumors.

cards.name: Wide local excision
cards.condition: PeIN, Ta, T1a lesions confined to skin with little invasion
cards.contraindications: Not recommended for T1b or higher, or when negative margins cannot be achieved
cards.detail: Excision of the penile lesion with wide negative margins; may include skin grafting. Local recurrence up to 50% at 2 years if margins inadequate. Wound infection, skin graft failure. Operative mortality: Low.

cards.name: Glansectomy
cards.condition: Distal tumors on glans or prepuce (PeIN, Ta, T1a); not recommended for pT1a unless needed for negative margins
cards.contraindications: T1b or higher, or when negative margins cannot be achieved with glansectomy alone
cards.detail: Removal of the glans penis; may be followed by split- or full-thickness skin graft for neoglans reconstruction. Local recurrence 2.6%–16.7%. Urinary complications, erectile dysfunction. Operative mortality: Low.

cards.name: Partial penectomy
cards.condition: High-grade primary tumors (≥pT1b or ≥T2) where functional stump can be preserved
cards.contraindications: When functional stump cannot be preserved or patient preference for total penectomy
cards.detail: Removal of the distal penis with preservation of a functional stump; intraoperative frozen sections for margins. Positive margins requiring re-resection, urinary fistula, sexual dysfunction. Operative mortality: Low.

cards.name: Total penectomy
cards.condition: When partial penectomy cannot achieve negative margins or for T4 tumors invading adjacent structures
cards.contraindications: When organ-sparing or partial penectomy is feasible
cards.detail: Complete removal of the penis; may include urethrostomy. Urinary diversion complications, psychological impact, sexual dysfunction. Operative mortality: Low.

cards.name: Inguinal lymph node dissection (ILND)
cards.condition: Staging or treatment of inguinal lymph node metastases; for intermediate/high-risk non-palpable nodes or palpable nodes
cards.detail: Standard or modified template; standard includes Daseler’s quadrilateral area. Lymphedema, wound infection, skin necrosis, seroma. Operative mortality: Low.

cards.name: Pelvic lymph node dissection (PLND)
cards.condition: ≥2 positive inguinal nodes on ipsilateral ILND or extranodal extension; ≥4 positive inguinal nodes (bilateral PLND)
cards.detail: Resection of external iliac, internal iliac, and obturator lymph nodes; unilateral or bilateral. Lymphedema, vascular injury, infection. Operative mortality: Low.
